settings.text: Care for checkups, changes, and everything between.
Use this page as a starting point. If you are not sure which appointment your pet needs, call and we will help you decide.

settings.title: Common reasons pets visit us.
settings.description: Most appointments fit one of these everyday care categories.

settings.subtitle: Prevention
settings.title: Wellness plans
settings.description: Routine exams, vaccine schedules, nutrition notes, and parasite prevention.

settings.subtitle: New pets
settings.title: Puppy and kitten visits
settings.description: Starter vaccines, microchip conversations, diet guidance, and early behavior questions.

settings.subtitle: Illness
settings.title: Sick care
settings.description: Support for vomiting, diarrhea, limping, ear issues, coughing, and appetite changes.

settings.subtitle: Dental
settings.title: Teeth and gums
settings.description: Oral exams, dental cleanings, and practical prevention at home.

settings.subtitle: Testing
settings.title: Lab work
settings.description: Bloodwork, urine checks, parasite screening, and monitoring for chronic conditions.

settings.subtitle: Imaging
settings.title: X-rays and imaging
settings.description: When an exam needs more information, imaging helps us see what cannot be felt.

settings.subtitle: Procedures
settings.title: Surgery
settings.description: Routine procedures with pre-visit instructions, estimates, and go-home care notes.

settings.subtitle: Support
settings.title: Behavior guidance
settings.description: Conversations around anxiety, new habits, age-related changes, and comfort at home.

settings.title: Care changes as pets grow.
settings.description: A good plan looks different for a new kitten, a healthy adult dog, and a senior cat with new stiffness.

settings.title: Puppy and kitten starts
settings.description: Vaccines, nutrition, early socialization, and prevention habits from the beginning.

settings.title: Adult health maintenance
settings.description: Annual exams, dental screening, weight conversations, and lifestyle-specific prevention.

settings.title: Senior comfort and monitoring
settings.description: Mobility, pain, kidney, thyroid, and behavior changes are easier to manage when tracked early.

settings.question: Can you give prices before the appointment?
settings.answer: We can share typical visit ranges by phone. Your veterinarian will explain recommendations and estimates before treatment begins.

settings.question: Do you need my pet's records?
settings.answer: Records help us understand vaccine history, medications, allergies, and previous test results. Bring them or ask your prior clinic to send them ahead.

settings.question: What counts as urgent?
settings.answer: Trouble breathing, collapse, repeated vomiting, toxin exposure, severe pain, seizures, or major injury should be treated as urgent. Call the clinic or local emergency hospital right away.

settings.question: Do you see cats?
settings.answer: Yes. We see cats and dogs, and we can help with carrier tips if your cat is nervous about travel.

settings.title: Not sure what to book?
settings.text: Call (555) 555-0100 and tell us what you are noticing. We will help route you to the right appointment.
Call us before booking if you are unsure whether your pet needs a wellness, sick, or urgent visit.
